old_claim.note: Stock Ticker Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No stock-ticker dependency remains because the scoped 1840s newsrooms circulated received information before the repository's 1863 stock-ticker system.

source_shape.source_locator: Telegraphs were big business describes Electric Telegraph Company newsrooms distributing news, stock information, and weather through the telegraph network.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The service was a telegraphic newsroom that handled several information categories rather than a stock-ticker mechanism.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Market prices were content carried by the room; a later electromechanical ticker was not required to receive or announce them.

ontology_before: A 1840s information service depended on a later 1863 stock-ticker machine because both handled market data.
ontology_after: Telegraphic newsrooms depend directly on telegraph communication; stock tickers remain a separate later display mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: telegraph_news_tickers no longer directly depends on stock_ticker_systems.
would_reject_if: The node were respecified as a mechanically printed stock-and-news ticker introduced after 1863.
short_reason: Stock information content does not require a later ticker machine.
